title: Algorand API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary and key terms for the Algorand blockchain REST APIs (Algod, Indexer, KMD)

terms.definition: The Algorand protocol daemon that exposes a REST API for real-time node interaction including submitting transactions, querying account state, and reading block data.
The Algorand Indexer daemon that provides historical ledger search via a PostgreSQL-backed REST API, enabling deep queries across transactions, accounts, assets, and applications.
Key Management Daemon that provides local wallet storage, key generation, and transaction signing for Algorand node operators.
Algorand Standard Asset — a native on-chain asset primitive representing fungible or non-fungible tokens with creator-defined parameters.
Transaction Execution Approval Language — the stack-based bytecode language used to write Algorand smart contracts (approval and clear-state programs).
Algorand Virtual Machine — the execution environment for TEAL programs that runs approval and clear-state programs for smart contracts.
An Algorand smart contract deployed on-chain, identified by an application ID. It has approval and clear-state programs and optionally global and local state storage.
A single block in the Algorand blockchain, identified by its round number. Algorand produces approximately one block every 3.3 seconds.
The official Algorand developer toolkit providing CLI tooling, a local development sandbox (LocalNet), and utility SDKs for Python and TypeScript.
The HTTP header used to authenticate requests to the Algod REST API when running a private or restricted node.
The HTTP header used to authenticate requests to the KMD REST API.
A snapshot of the Algorand ledger state at a specific round, used for fast node bootstrapping via catchup rather than replaying all blocks.
A cryptographic proof of the Algorand blockchain state at a given round, enabling light-client verification and cross-chain bridging.
Per-application key-value storage on Algorand that allows smart contracts to store arbitrary byte data beyond the limits of global and local state.
The smallest unit of ALGO, the native currency of the Algorand blockchain. 1 ALGO equals 1,000,000 microalgos.
A third-party provider of free and commercial Algorand archival Algod and Indexer API endpoints with global infrastructure.
Algorand uses Pure Proof-of-Stake (PPoS) consensus where block proposers and committee members are selected randomly proportional to their ALGO stake.
A pagination cursor returned by Indexer list responses. Pass this value as the next-token query parameter to fetch the next page of results.
The process by which an Algorand account accepts an ASA or application by sending a zero-transfer transaction, establishing the associated local state.
Algorand Algod API feature allowing developers to simulate a transaction group without submitting it, returning execution traces and results for debugging.
